2606.15207.title: Controlled Dynamics Attractor Transformer
2606.15207.paper_summary: Transformer architectures have dramatically advanced representation learning and inference in deep models through self-attention mechanisms. In parallel,associative memory (AM) frameworks map representations onto energy landscapes, offering interpretable retrieval mechanisms. However, their continuous-time inference dynamics lack the biological plausibility of classical Continuous Attractor Neural Networks (CANNs). To bridge this gap, we propose Controlled Dynamics Attractor Transformer (CDAT), which couples a mixture von Mises-Fisher (Mo-vMF) attention energy with a Hopfield refinement energy, while augmenting energy descent with a CANN-inspired excitation-inhibition modulation. CDAT instantiates a topology-constrained dynamical system whose couplings encode relational structure among tokens, thereby linking attractor-style dynamics to modern energy-based attention. We further provide a constructive dissipation analysis to formally establish their controlled inference dynamics. Benefiting from these robust and structured dynamics, CDAT achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple benchmarks in graph anomaly detection and graph classification.
2606.15207.paper_summary_zh: Transformer 架構透過自注意力機制顯著推進了深度模型中的表示學習和推理。<lb/>與此同時，聯想記憶 (AM) 框架將表示映射到能量景觀，提供可解釋的檢索機制。<lb/>然而，它們的連續時間推理動態缺乏經典連續吸引子神經網絡 (CANNs) 的生物學合理性。<lb/>為了彌補這一差距，我們提出了控制動態吸引子Transformer (CDAT)，它將混合的 von Mises-Fisher (Mo-vMF) 注意力能量與 Hopfield 精煉能量結合，同時通過受 CANN 啟發的興奮-抑制調制來增強能量下降。<lb/>CDAT 實現了一個拓撲約束的動態系統，其耦合編碼了令牌之間的關係結構，從而將吸引子風格的動態與現代基於能量的注意力聯繫起來。<lb/>我們進一步提供了一個建設性的耗散分析，以正式建立它們的受控推理動態。<lb/>得益於這些穩健且結構化的動態，CDAT 在圖異常檢測和圖分類的多個基準中實現了最先進的性能。
2606.15207.author: Cheng Zhang et.al.
2606.15207.authors: Cheng Zhang, Minnan Luo, Zesheng Yang, Ming Li, Yong-Jin Liu, Qinghua Zheng
